summary: Cleaned‑up soundboard captures an early‑’69 psychedelic marathon with a standout Viola → Caution jam and the experimental “What’s Become of the Baby” backing tape.
review: The recording is a well‑pitched, cleaned‑up soundboard that sounds clear and balanced for most of the show, though a few sections (e.g., cymbal echo on “Morning Dew”) are a bit muddy. Musically the concert is a wild early‑’69 set – Jerry’s psychedelic stretch through “Dupree’s”, “Mountains of the Moon”, “Dark Star Jam” and “China Cat” is electric, while the second half delivers the legendary Viola → Caution jam, an epic “What’s Become of the Baby” tape experiment and a superb rendition of “The Other One”. Overall the performance is praised despite some sloppy moments in “Hard to Handle” and occasional rhythm‑section messes.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.44★; most comments highlight strong musical moments and good soundboard quality, with only minor criticisms about occasional sloppiness.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cleaned‑up and correctly pitched by Miller; generally clear and balanced. Some listeners note a slightly muddy cymbal echo on “Morning Dew” and occasional muddiness, but overall the sound is very listenable.

standout_songs: What's Become of the Baby (backing tape)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑’69 psychedelic first set (Dupree’s, Mountains of the Moon → Dark Star Jam → China Cat → Doin’ That Rag) segues into a Pig Pen‑heavy middle, then erupts in an experimental second set featuring the famed backing tape and long jams like Viola–Caution and The Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Leads on blues‑style numbers, switches from acoustic to electric, and weaves wild, willowy leads throughout the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Phil: Provides backup vocals (e.g., on “Silver Threads”) and is highlighted during the Viola segment; his bass underpins the long improvisations.
band_member_comments.Bob: His voice appears on “Baby Blue”, which rocks out despite being over‑played later; also contributes to the high energy of the encore.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming is described as a bit sloppy in places (e.g., “Hard to Handle”) but drives the expansive jams effectively.
